slides.title: The reason you *can't stop snacking*.
slides.sub: It's called the protein leverage hypothesis: eat too little protein and your appetite won't quit until you hit your target.

slides.title: Your body hunts protein
slides.body: The *protein leverage hypothesis* says you keep eating until you meet your protein need — even past your calories.

slides.title: Snacks are mostly carbs
slides.body: Low-protein snacks barely move the needle, so the hunger signal stays on and you reach for *more and more*.

slides.title: Anchor protein first
slides.body: Build meals around protein and the craving switch flips off sooner. Aim for *0.7–1g per lb* of goal weight.

slides.title: Start the day high
slides.body: A *30g protein breakfast* blunts afternoon snacking better than any willpower hack.

slides.title: Fewer calories, no fight
slides.body: Hit your protein and total intake often drops on its own. *Appetite does the work* for you.

slides.title: Stop snacking *on autopilot*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health counts your protein for you — so you know the moment you've hit your number.

caption:
If you graze all afternoon and never feel full, it might not be willpower. 🍽️

The protein leverage hypothesis says your body keeps eating until it hits its protein need, so anchor meals around protein and front-load breakfast. Save this one. 🔖

Vibe Health counts your protein from a photo so you actually know when you're there → link in bio.
